name: First Baptist Church (Stratford)
description_short: A small federal credit union in Stratford, CT serving 215 members with basic banking and lending services. Established in 1974 with $345K in assets.
description_long:
First Baptist Church (Stratford) Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution that has served the Stratford, Connecticut community for over 50 years since its founding in 1974. As a federal credit union with an associational-religious membership type, it caters specifically to members affiliated with or connected to First Baptist Church and the surrounding community. The institution operates as a single-branch operation headquartered at 40 Johnson Court in Stratford, with a relatively modest membership base of approximately 215 members and total assets of $345,268.

The credit union offers essential financial services including checking and savings accounts, ATM and debit card programs, and various loan products. Their lending portfolio consists of $62,117 in total loans, including unsecured personal loans ($15,449), other loan categories ($46,668), and a small allowance for loan losses ($849). The organization actively participates in the Payday Alternative Loans (PAL) program, which provides members with small-dollar loans at competitive rates as an alternative to predatory payday lending. Their deposit base of $319,095 is held in regular shares ($304,881) and other deposit accounts ($14,214).

What distinguishes this credit union is its deeply local, faith-based character and member-focused mission. Operating as a small associational credit union tied to a religious institution rather than a workplace or geographic charter, it emphasizes personal relationships and community accountability. Leadership is stable, with Norma Tate-Perry serving as CEO/Manager and Joan B Davis as Chairperson. The organization maintains NCUA federal oversight with Charter Number 21614 and passed its most recent audit on December 1, 2024.

However, prospective members should understand the significant limitations of this institution. With only $345K in total assets and 215 members, this is an extremely small credit union with minimal operational scale. The financial statements reveal modest profitability (net income of $627 in the most recent period) and a tight equity cushion ($26,149 total equity, or 7.6% of assets). Limited membership eligibility—restricted to those with associational ties to First Baptist Church—makes this inaccessible to most consumers. The lack of a website and minimal digital presence suggests limited online banking capabilities. This credit union is best suited for existing or prospective church members seeking highly personalized, local banking rather than comprehensive financial services.
services: Savings accounts (regular shares and other deposit products)
In-person banking at single Stratford branch location
pros: Federal credit union status with NCUA insurance protection on deposits up to $250K
Actively offers Payday Alternative Loans (PAL) at rates under 36% APR as a predatory lending alternative
Extremely personalized service with direct access to leadership (CEO Norma Tate-Perry, Chairperson Joan B Davis)
Long operating history since 1974 demonstrates stability and institutional commitment to the Stratford community
Member-owned cooperative structure means profits benefit account holders rather than external shareholders
ATM and debit card access for basic transaction needs
Recently audited (December 1, 2024) demonstrating regulatory compliance and financial transparency
cons: Extremely limited asset base ($345K) creates operational constraints and minimal product sophistication
Restricted membership to those with First Baptist Church affiliations dramatically limits accessibility
No identifiable website or online banking presence limits remote account management capabilities
Very small membership (215 members) suggests limited branch services, hours, and staff expertise
Thin profit margins (net income of only $627) and modest equity ($26,149) indicate financial fragility
best_for: Current or prospective members of First Baptist Church (Stratford) seeking basic checking/savings accounts
Small-dollar borrowers looking for Payday Alternative Loans under 36% APR with local approval
Community members who value hyper-local, faith-based banking with personal relationship emphasis
Savers seeking federally insured deposits with support for a specific religious community institution
diagnosis: First Baptist Church (Stratford) Federal Credit Union is exclusively designed for members affiliated with its namesake religious institution and the immediate Stratford community. This is a microfinance institution best suited for existing church members or community members seeking highly personalized banking; the restricted membership model and minimal asset base make it inaccessible and impractical for most consumers seeking competitive rates, digital banking, or comprehensive financial products.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
